idea.hook: What if you fell into a quasar?
facts: A quasar's event horizon is 4 million miles wide, roughly the distance from NYC to LA and back
Quasars emit 10^40 photons per second, enough to power 100 billion Suns
The accretion disk of a quasar spins at 3,000 km/s, faster than a football field-sized object orbiting the Earth
idea.payoff: and we still don't know what's at their centers
idea.title: Quasar Fall 🌀

scenes.text: What if you fell into a Quasar?
scenes.visual: Distant view of a swirling galaxy, slowly zooming in towards a bright, intense central core.
Close-up of a quasar's vast, dark event horizon with gravitational lensing distortion. Text highlights the immense circular boundary.
Intense, glowing streams of light and radiation bursting from the quasar's core, illustrating its immense energy output.
A vibrant, rapidly spinning accretion disk with streaks of superheated matter, emphasizing extreme velocity.
The quasar visual slowly recedes, becoming a mysterious, enigmatic point of light against a deep, star-filled cosmic background, emphasizing the unknown.
